full_text_rationale: Full text describes KernelSkill's two-level memory architecture: a retrievable long-term kernel-optimization knowledge memory reused across kernels and a short-term trajectory memory updated across refinement rounds, with KernelBench evaluation and memory ablations.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 010; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Qitong Sun, Jun Han, Tianlin Li, Zhe Tang, Sheng Chen, Fei Yang, Aishan Liu, Xianglong Liu, Yang Liu
titulo: KernelSkill: A Multi-Agent Framework for GPU Kernel Optimization
formato_armazenamento: Dual-level memory: long-term expert skill memory with standardized profiling fields, code features, bottleneck rules, decision tables, and method knowledge; short-term repair and optimization trajectory memory recording plans, compiler/verifier/profiler feedback, and kernel versions.
framework_agente: KernelSkill multi-agent GPU kernel optimization framework with generator, compiler, verifier, profiler, diagnoser, repairer, planner, and optimizer roles
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; ablation; refinement-efficiency analysis; baseline comparison
benchmarks_utilizados: KernelBench Levels 1-3 with 250 tasks; NVIDIA A100 80GB; CUDA 12.4; cuDNN 9.1.0; cuBLAS 12.9
metricas_desempenho: success rate, Fast1, average runtime speedup over Torch Eager, per-round speedup, correctness verification, latency with warmup and timed CUDA events
metricas_cl: Long-term memory ablation, short-term memory ablation, no-memory ablation, cross-task reusable skill selection, per-task trajectory-state effect
baseline_comparado: Kevin-32B, Astra, PRAGMA, CudaForge, QiMeng, STARK, Torch Eager, and KernelSkill ablations
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Long-term schema stores expert optimization skills and decision rules; short-term memory is updated across up to 15 refinement rounds per task. Each task samples 3 seed kernels and promotes new base kernels only after relative or absolute speedup thresholds.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; experiments report hardware, candidate seeds, and up to 15 refinement rounds per task but not monetary cost.
resultado_principal: KernelSkill achieves 100% success on KernelBench Levels 1-3 and average speedups of 5.44x, 2.82x, and 1.92x over Torch Eager.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Compared with STARK, KernelSkill improves average speedup by 79.5% on Level 1, 4.8% on Level 2, and 21.5% on Level 3. It reaches these gains with 15 refinement rounds, while STARK reports 30 rounds.
custo_vs_baseline: KernelSkill reports stronger speedup per refinement round than STARK: 0.36/0.19/0.13 on Levels 1/2/3 versus 0.10/0.09/0.05 for STARK.
limitacoes: Some baselines are not open-sourced, so several comparisons use reported results or reimplementations rather than a single identical codebase; evaluation is limited to KernelBench GPU-kernel tasks.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on NVIDIA A100 hardware, profiling tools, seed selection, fixed refinement rounds, threshold settings, ChatGPT-5.1 behavior, and comparability of non-open-source baseline results.
